name: PDE isoenzyme selectivity differences limit adverse-effect and mechanism transfer
statement: The PDE5 inhibitors share the target enzyme but differ in off-target PDE selectivity. CIALIS labeling notes tadalafil is much more selective for PDE5 than PDE6 but only 14-fold more potent for PDE5 than PDE11A1; VIAGRA and vardenafil labels highlight PDE6-related visual physiology; STENDRA labeling reports avanafil is more than 100-fold more potent for PDE5 than PDE6 and more than 10,000-fold more potent than several other PDEs including PDE11.
population_or_context: Mechanistic and label selectivity evidence for deciding when adverse-effect, ocular, musculoskeletal, vascular, or tissue-specific findings can transfer across agents.
quantitative_note: Tadalafil: 700-fold more potent for PDE5 than PDE6 and 14-fold versus PDE11A1 in label. Sildenafil: about 10-fold as potent for PDE5 compared with PDE6 in label. Vardenafil: more than 15-fold relative to PDE6 and more than 300-fold relative to PDE11 in label. Avanafil: greater than 100-fold relative to PDE6 and greater than 10,000-fold relative to PDE1, PDE3, PDE9, and PDE11 in label.

source_locator.section: Bischoff abstract; DailyMed clinical pharmacology mechanism/selectivity sections
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: E-utilities metadata and search abstract snippet were combined with label selectivity values from CIALIS, VIAGRA, vardenafil, and STENDRA.

caveats: In vitro selectivity does not directly quantify clinical adverse-event risk.
Label selectivity values do not prove one agent is globally safer for off-label use.
Different endpoints may depend on different tissues and PDE isoenzymes.
population_profile: Mechanistic and label evidence, not a healthy-user clinical endpoint study.
tadalafil_exposure: Mixed PDE5 inhibitor selectivity; tadalafil is direct only for the tadalafil/PDE11 and PDE6 comparison.
class_transfer_limits: Do not transfer visual, musculoskeletal, ocular, retinal, or tissue-specific mechanistic findings across agents without checking PDE6/PDE11 and other isoenzyme selectivity plus exposure context.
indication_context: Mechanistic pharmacology and regulatory label selectivity evidence.
endpoint_measure: Relative potency/selectivity for PDE5 versus PDE6, PDE11, and other phosphodiesterases.
effect_estimate: Qualitative with label selectivity ratios showing agent differences.
safety_findings: Selectivity differences support keeping visual and musculoskeletal safety caveats agent-specific unless a class source directly evaluates the outcome.
directness_to_target_population: High for agent-transfer rules; low for estimating clinical event rates in healthy men age 30 to 50.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Review-level and in vitro/selectivity data can be overinterpreted if mapped directly to clinical outcomes.
